questions.question: What two firsts did Flamingo (2022) demonstrate?
options: First open-source VLM and first GPU training
Processing arbitrarily interleaved image/video/text sequences, and in-context few-shot multimodal learning — caption a new image from three example pairs with no gradient step
First model to beat ImageNet and first chatbot
Text-to-image generation and speech synthesis
questions.explanation: Both capabilities flowed from one architectural idea: leave the frozen LLM's stream untouched and insert gated cross-attention between its blocks. It's the ancestor of Gemini's interleaved inputs.

questions.question: Why does Flamingo's gate use tanh(alpha) with alpha initialized to zero?
options: Tanh is the fastest activation
tanh(0) = 0, so at initialization the new cross-attention layers are no-ops — the model IS the pretrained Chinchilla on text; visual conditioning grows smoothly and additively as alpha opens
Zero initialization saves memory
It prevents overfitting on small data
questions.explanation: The single most important design choice: visual conditioning is additive, gated, and zero at start. The residual means even a fully-open gate adds visual information on top rather than overwriting text capability.

questions.question: What does the Perceiver resampler guarantee about visual token counts?
options: More patches always produce more tokens
K=64 fixed learnable latents cross-attend over however many patches arrive — a 196-patch and a 900-patch image both exit as exactly 64 tokens, so the LLM cross-attention always sees the same shape
Tokens are compressed to exactly one per image
Token count equals the caption length
questions.explanation: Six blocks of (latents cross-attend patches, then self-attention + FFN among latents). For video, per-frame latents plus temporal positions give T×64 tokens — variable input, fixed interface.

questions.question: In an interleaved prompt "<image A> caption A <image B> caption B <image C> ?", what does the cross-attention mask enforce?
options: Every text token sees every image
A text token attends only to images that precede it — Flamingo specifically chose "only the most recent preceding image"
Images attend to each other directly
Only the final token sees any image
questions.explanation: Causal masking extended to image placement keeps few-shot structure intact: each caption is conditioned on its own image, which is what makes the 3-examples-then-query prompt pattern work.

questions.question: How does Flamingo's bridge differ architecturally from BLIP-2's?
options: They are identical
BLIP-2 prepends compressed visual tokens to the LLM's INPUT; Flamingo inserts gated cross-attention layers BETWEEN the frozen LLM's blocks, leaving the input stream purely textual
Flamingo fine-tunes the whole LLM
BLIP-2 has no vision encoder
questions.explanation: Input-level injection (BLIP-2/LLaVA style) vs interleaved-layer injection (Flamingo style) is the fundamental fork in VLM bridge design — with different costs in parameters, context budget, and multi-image handling.
